head.hi: Statement by the Spanish Government Published in “Gaceta de Madrid,” November 12, 1927

div.p: The Government of His Majesty, accepting the proposal made to it by that of the United States has agreed to continue applying temporarily to the products of the latter country, from the 27th of the present month of November, most favored nation treatment as granted them by the Royal Decree of May 25th last, which regime will cease three months from its denouncement or upon the conclusion of a new commercial agreement between both countries.
Which I make public for general knowledge.
div.closer:
Madrid, November 7, 1927.
Secretary General,
B. Almeida
